brands.name: Rheem
brands.notes: Primary residential HVAC brand — Classic, Classic Plus, Prestige product lines

brands.name: Ruud
brands.notes: Near-total Rheem platform alias — typically different distribution channel. Ruud models are essentially Rheem with different badge/first letter.

brands.name: WeatherKing
brands.notes: Rheem value/distributor-exclusive sub-brand. Aliases Rheem platforms with reduced-feature variants on shared IFC boards.

structural_overview: Rheem Manufacturing Company's US residential HVAC diagnostics span four distinct platforms with fragmented public documentation: (1) LEGACY SINGLE-LED/DUAL-LED value-line furnaces (90RS/90RT/90TS and close aliases) — uses amber flame LED for flame quality states (off/rapid/slow/steady) plus green status LED blink codes (1-5 blinks). (2) MODERN 7-SEGMENT furnace with THREE DISTINCT NAMESPACES within this platform: (2A) Endeavor-style numeric (W801T/R801T UT 1194-250 boards) — status letters O/C/H/F plus numeric codes 10-93; (2B) Axxx_F/Txxx_F premium/Bluetooth namespace (RDFXYC and communicating-capable premium family) — detailed troubleshooting deferred to contractor app via QR code; (2C) Older RGFD modulating numeric-function-code generation — codes 11-67 with function-code diagnostic guide structure. Model prefix alone is NOT sufficient to disambiguate which namespace applies; control generation / literature revision required. (3) ECONET COMMUNICATING ECOSYSTEM — EcoNet Control Center thermostat + communicating indoor/outdoor equipment. Strongest public dictionary is on the RH2T/RHMV air handler side (A101-A110 + 111). Control Center thermostat-only alarm dictionary is NOT publicly exposed as a complete code list. (4) PRESTIGE INVERTER OUTDOOR (RP18AZ-class Universal Outdoor Control / UODC) — hard numeric lockouts (21L low-pressure, 29L high-pressure) plus A-code namespace for persistent alarms and T-code namespace for transient/strike-count-escalating alarms. Rheem publishes actual sensor-validation math for suction and liquid transducers. 21L low-pressure lockout in heating has a unique auto-reset only when outdoor ambient rises to -10°F. Active System Protection monitors power/torque/current/voltage/discharge-pressure/temp and reduces inverter speed BEFORE hard shutdown.

field_quirks.quirk: Legacy 4 ignition trials or 4 flame failures in one heat call trigger 1-hour soft lockout; reset by 115V interrupt OR thermostat off 5-10 seconds
field_quirks.reason: Rheem's public Gas Furnace FAQ and 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3 both confirm this: 4 failed ignition trials OR 4 flame failures during one heat call trigger the 1-blink soft lockout state. Reset options are (a) cycle 115V power to furnace OR (b) turn thermostat off 5-10 seconds and back on. Between the 2nd and 3rd ignition trials, the main blower and inducer run for 180 seconds as a purge period. Using the wrong reset window (outside 5-10 seconds) fails to clear the lockout and the tech wastes time chasing phantom faults.

field_quirks.quirk: Modern numeric '10' is a lockout WRAPPER, not a root fault — display alternates 10 with underlying cause code
field_quirks.applies_to: Endeavor-style 7-segment boards (W801T, R801T, UT Electronic Controls 1194-250 family)
field_quirks.reason: Per W801T Installation Instructions lockout section: when the control is in 1-hour soft lockout, the 7-segment display alternates between '10' and the actual underlying fault code (11 failed ignition, 13 flame lost, 14 undesired flame, 61 dead blower, etc.). A technician who reads only '10' is seeing the wrapper, not the cause. Always wait for the display to cycle and capture both numbers. Reset by cycling heat call off >2 sec but <20 sec, or power off minimum 5 seconds, or auto-reset after 1 hour.

field_quirks.quirk: Blocked drain switch on upflow 90+ condensing furnaces is in series with gas valve — drain issues mimic flame failure lockouts
field_quirks.applies_to: Upflow Rheem/Ruud 90+ condensing furnaces (legacy platform and older modern platform)
field_quirks.reason: Rheem's public Gas Furnace FAQ states the blocked drain switch on upflow 90+ models is wired in series with the gas valve. A condensate drain issue (clogged trap, blocked drain line, frozen condensate) de-energizes the gas valve, which the control sees as repeated flame failures. After 4 flame failures in one call, the furnace enters 1-hour soft lockout. The fault code indicates flame-sense issue but the ACTUAL cause is condensate management. Always inspect the drain path BEFORE chasing flame sensor or gas valve on any repeating flame failure in an upflow 90+ unit.

field_quirks.quirk: EcoNet bus must be wired daisy-chain, NOT star topology
field_quirks.applies_to: All EcoNet communicating systems (Control Center + communicating furnaces/air handlers/outdoor units)
field_quirks.reason: Rheem and Ruud Control Center support pages explicitly require daisy-chain wiring of the EcoNet communication bus between devices. Star topology (thermostat to each device independently) will cause intermittent communication failures, dropped alerts, and false equipment alarms. Terminals R, C, E1, E2 carry power and data. Max 125 ft between communicating controls, 500 ft end-to-end. A tech troubleshooting 'no comm' on EcoNet should always verify the physical wiring topology first before assuming component failure.

field_quirks.quirk: EcoNet enforces 4-minute anti-short-cycle delay after power-up or compressor shutdown
field_quirks.applies_to: All EcoNet Control Center installations with communicating cooling or heat-pump heating
field_quirks.reason: Ruud Control Center Support page: cooling and heat-pump heating will NOT operate for 4 minutes after power is applied to the system OR after the outdoor unit compressor shuts off. A 'no start' customer complaint within this window is normal protective behavior, not a fault. Confirm elapsed time before diagnosing.

field_quirks.quirk: EcoNet mode IGNORES DIP-switch airflow settings on RH2T/RHMV air handlers — airflow adjusted via thermostat/Control Center
field_quirks.applies_to: RH2T, RHMV, and other EcoNet-capable communicating air handlers operating in communicating mode
field_quirks.reason: Rheem RH2T/RHMV Installation Instructions 92-20521-102 explicitly states that when the air handler is operating in EcoNet communicating mode (wired with E1/E2 data wires), the board's DIP-switch airflow adjustments are completely ignored. All airflow trim must be performed through the EcoNet Control Center service interface. A technician who changes DIP switches to 'fix' airflow on a communicating install will see zero change until they either revert to non-communicating mode OR use the thermostat menus.

field_quirks.quirk: Prestige inverter 21L low-pressure lockout in heating auto-resets ONLY when outdoor ambient rises to -10°F
field_quirks.reason: Rheem RP18AZ Installation Instructions low-suction-pressure section: 21L low-refrigerant-pressure lockout normally requires manual reset (power off both indoor and outdoor units for 1 minute). BUT if the lockout occurred in heating mode AND the outdoor ambient was below -10°F at the time, the system will auto-reset when ambient rises back up to -10°F or above. This is the ONLY auto-reset exception documented. In cooling mode OR at any ambient above -10°F, 21L stays locked until manual reset.

field_quirks.quirk: Prestige inverter Active System Protection reduces compressor speed BEFORE hard shutdown — the system manages its own envelope proactively
field_quirks.applies_to: RP18AZ-class Prestige/Ultra inverter outdoor units with UODC (Universal Outdoor Control)
field_quirks.reason: Rheem publishes that the inverter continuously monitors compressor power, torque, current, voltage, discharge pressure, and discharge temperature. When conditions approach envelope limits, the UODC reduces compressor RPM to keep operation safe BEFORE triggering a fault. Example: discharge line temp rises to 235°F triggers RPM reduction; only if temp stays high does the system hard-fault with T916_O. This means intermittent fault codes often indicate the system is ALREADY self-correcting; the fix is usually the underlying condition (charge, airflow, coil cleanliness) not the inverter itself.

field_quirks.quirk: Three distinct 7-segment furnace namespaces within Platform 2 — model prefix does NOT disambiguate, control generation required
field_quirks.applies_to: All Rheem/Ruud modern furnaces with 7-segment displays — cannot identify which namespace applies from model number alone
field_quirks.reason: Platform 2A Endeavor numeric (W801T/R801T with UT 1194-250 boards): status O/C/H/F + codes 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 20, 22, 26, 33, 55, 57-59, 61, 93. Platform 2B Axxx_F/Txxx_F premium Bluetooth (RDFXYC): A011_F, T013_F, A014_F, A031_F, etc. with QR-code contractor app. Platform 2C Older RGFD modulating: codes 11, 12, 13, 14, 22/23/33, 44/45/46/57, 67 with function-code troubleshooting guide. A technician seeing 'code 11' on a Rheem furnace needs to confirm WHICH generation board is installed before picking the right diagnostic path. Literature revision date + board part number are the disambiguators, not model prefix.

field_quirks.quirk: Rheem contractor app (Bluetooth + QR code) holds the detailed troubleshooting trees for Axxx_F/Txxx_F premium furnaces — the printed booklet intentionally references it
field_quirks.applies_to: RDFXYC Bluetooth-capable premium furnaces and newer communicating/R-454B-capable generations
field_quirks.reason: RDFXYC Installation Instructions 92-110869-01-01 publicly state that detailed fault troubleshooting information is in the QR-linked contractor app. The booklet includes the fault-code identifiers and names but deliberately pushes the step-by-step diagnostic trees into the app. This matters because a tech without the app has significantly less information on newer equipment than on older 7-segment Endeavor boards where the booklet has the full tree. Expect this pattern to accelerate on R-454B and later generations.

field_quirks.quirk: Some modern furnaces keep a 3-fault buffer (faults <1 week old) that flashes after power reset; DIP-switch toggle within 30 seconds clears memory
field_quirks.applies_to: Select modern Rheem/Ruud furnace boards with fault-buffer behavior
field_quirks.reason: Public Rheem furnace literature documents: the 3 most recent faults less than one week old are flashed on the red LED after power reset. The fault memory can be cleared by toggling the relevant DIP switch sequence within 30 seconds. This matters because a tech cycling power to 'reset' a furnace sees the most recent faults flash by — if they miss the sequence, they lose diagnostic information. The 30-second clear window is also easy to trigger accidentally by installers doing DIP switch setup.

field_quirks.quirk: EcoNet Control Center autodiscovers indoor and outdoor equipment within approximately 45 seconds of all devices being powered and connected correctly
field_quirks.applies_to: All EcoNet Control Center installations during commissioning
field_quirks.reason: Ruud Control Center Support: the Control Center should detect all communicating indoor and outdoor equipment within ~45 seconds once all devices are powered and connected with correct daisy-chain topology. If after 45 seconds the Control Center has NOT discovered all equipment, the installer should check (a) daisy-chain vs star topology, (b) shield grounding at one point only (NOT at Control Center end), (c) wire damage, (d) 125 ft max between devices, (e) 500 ft end-to-end. Knowing the 45-second window keeps techs from prematurely declaring 'comm fault' during normal autodiscovery.

field_quirks.quirk: EcoNet communication wiring shield must be grounded at ONE point only — NOT at Control Center end; multi-point grounding creates current loops and degrades communication
field_quirks.applies_to: All EcoNet communicating system installations using shielded thermostat wire
field_quirks.reason: Rheem/Ruud EcoNet install literature specifies: control wiring shielding must be grounded in ONE location only, with the shield NOT connected at the Control Center end. Multi-point grounding creates current loops that degrade communication quality and cause intermittent A100_O (comm lost) and related comm faults. This is a wiring-quality issue that looks exactly like component failure unless the tech knows to check the shield grounding topology.

field_quirks.quirk: Rheem warns NOT to use phone cord for EcoNet control wiring
field_quirks.reason: Rheem/Ruud public EcoNet docs explicitly warn against using phone cord for EcoNet communicating wiring. Phone cord lacks shielding and has insufficient conductor sizing for reliable EcoNet communication. Using phone cord produces symptoms identical to bad equipment — intermittent comm loss, dropped alerts, autodiscovery failures — but the fix is rewire with proper shielded thermostat cable.

field_quirks.quirk: RH2T/RHMV communicating air handlers have RJ-14 diagnostic tool port for service
field_quirks.applies_to: RH2T, RHMV, and related EcoNet communicating air handlers
field_quirks.reason: Public RH2T/RHMV Installation Instructions confirm an RJ-14 diagnostic tool port on the air-handler control board. Techs with the Rheem service tool can plug in for direct equipment diagnostics without needing the Control Center or the Rheem Contractor App. This is a backup diagnostic path for situations where the EcoNet bus is compromised or the thermostat is disconnected.

field_quirks.quirk: RH2T/RHMV CFM LED flashes 1 flash per 100 CFM — field verification aid for airflow troubleshooting
field_quirks.applies_to: RH2T, RHMV constant-CFM communicating air handlers with CFM LED
field_quirks.reason: Public RH2T/RHMV docs: on communicating constant-CFM motors, the onboard CFM LED flashes at a rate of 1 flash per 100 CFM of actual airflow. 800 CFM = 8 flashes. 1200 CFM = 12 flashes. This gives the tech a real-time airflow verification tool directly on the equipment without needing a manometer or external CFM meter. Especially useful for verifying EcoNet airflow changes actually took effect (since DIP switches are ignored in communicating mode).

field_quirks.quirk: R-454B leak detection system (LDS) must remain electrically powered at ALL times after installation
field_quirks.applies_to: Rheem R-454B residential equipment with integrated refrigerant leak detection
field_quirks.reason: Public Rheem R-454B documentation: the leak detection system must be electrically powered at all times after installation for the system to be effective. Powering down the LDS defeats its purpose — it can no longer detect refrigerant leaks. Some Rheem R-454B indoor equipment requires service to restore operability after an A2L leak-detection event triggers. Older R-410A and R-22 indoor coils typically LACK the required refrigerant detection system and may not be compatible with R-454B outdoor units without coil replacement.

platforms.platform_name: Rheem / Ruud Legacy Single-LED / Dual-LED Flash
platforms.platform_description: Rheem and Ruud legacy value-line residential gas furnace platform with dual-LED integrated furnace control: amber flame LED for flame-quality status (off / rapid blink / slow blink / steady on) plus green status LED for fault indication (1-5 blinks). Covers 90RS/90RT/90TS value-line and close aliases. Published flash legend in public 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3 plus Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ. Ignition lockout: 4 trials, 1-hour auto-reset, manual reset by 115V interrupt or thermostat off 5-10 seconds. Blocked drain switch in series with gas valve on upflow 90+ models — a known misdiagnosis trap where drain issues surface as flame failure lockouts.
platforms.visual_identification: Dual-LED integrated furnace control: amber flame LED (signals flame quality through off/rapid/slow/steady states) and green status LED (signals faults through 1-5 blink patterns). No digital display on this platform. Brochure-style printed code legend typically on inside of furnace access panel or control board sticker.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3 plus Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ. Public coverage is concise legend + FAQ root causes — accessible public snippet does not expose per-code detailed diagnostic flowcharts the way Platform 2A Endeavor does. Records below honestly flag this where applicable.

diagnostic_path: Verify whether there is an active call for heat
If burners should be lit but amber LED is OFF, continue through ignition troubleshooting and flame-sense checks in the furnace troubleshooting chart
codes.escalation: Status-only indication; escalation depends on accompanying green status blink code
codes.reset_behavior: No reset needed; indication changes automatically with flame status.
codes.source: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3, troubleshooting legend: 'FLAME (AMBER) LED CODES: OFF = No Flame Present'

diagnostic_path: Verify whether flame is physically present when no call should exist
If no real flame is present, continue with flame-sense and gas-valve circuit checks in OEM troubleshooting
codes.escalation: Can participate in soft-lockout behavior when undesired flame is detected; accessible legacy snippet does not publish separate timing rule on this LED entry itself
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when unexpected-flame condition is removed; if lockout has occurred, use published lockout reset procedure.
codes.source: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3, flame LED legend

diagnostic_path: Check polarity/phasing and equipment grounding
Inspect and clean flame rod
Verify manifold pressure and flame carryover
Verify wiring continuity between flame rod and IFC
codes.escalation: Advisory/status until flame signal falls below minimum threshold or contributes to flame-loss/ignition-failure lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Clears automatically when flame signal rises above threshold; lockout reset only if a lockout condition also occurred.
codes.source: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3, flame LED legend and flame-sense troubleshooting notes; Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ flame-sense causes

root_causes: Normal burner flame established and flame rectification present
diagnostic_path: If burners are lit and flame LED is steady, flame sensing is normal; continue troubleshooting elsewhere if there is still a system fault
codes.escalation: Normal status; no escalation by itself
codes.reset_behavior: No reset needed.
codes.source: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3, flame LED legend

root_causes: No flame sensed after four ignition trials
Four flame failures during one call for heat
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply and manifold pressure
Verify polarity and grounding
Inspect spark/ignitor alignment
Inspect flame sensor and flame carryover
Verify gas valve wiring and 24V at valve during trials
codes.escalation: System attempts ignition 4 times, then enters 1-hour soft lockout; main blower and inducer run 180 seconds between 2nd and 3rd trials; neither ignition control nor gas valve re-energizes until lockout expires or is reset
codes.reset_behavior: Turn off 115V power to furnace OR turn thermostat OFF for 5-10 seconds.
codes.source: Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ soft-lockout entry; Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3, troubleshooting chart and lockout note

root_causes: Blocked pressure-switch tubing or inducer port
diagnostic_path: Check negative pressure with Magnehelic
Inspect pressure-switch tube/port
Inspect venting
Inspect hoses and switch contacts
Inspect heat exchanger restriction if indicated
codes.escalation: Fault indication; on related later boards, prolonged proving failure becomes timed soft lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when pressure-switch circuit proves normally.
codes.source: Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ flash-code meanings and causes; value-line installation troubleshooting chart

root_causes: Open main high limit due to low airflow or overfired heat exchanger
Blocked-drain-switch interaction on upflow 90+ can also indirectly produce flame-failure/lockout behavior
diagnostic_path: Check filter/airflow restrictions
Inspect registers/dampers
Verify temperature rise and firing rate
Inspect rollout/manual-reset limits
Inspect flue/heat exchanger
Inspect limit wiring and continuity
codes.escalation: Heating sequence interrupted while limit circuit remains open; may participate in later one-hour lockout behavior on newer generations
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when limit circuit is restored; manual-reset rollout must be physically reset; blocked-drain issue requires trap/drain correction rather than blind board reset.
codes.source: Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ; Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions troubleshooting text

codes.meaning: Pressure switch circuit closed / pressure switch stuck closed
root_causes: Pressure-switch contacts remain closed during off cycle
Improper reopening from previous heat call
diagnostic_path: Verify switch opens in off cycle
Inspect contacts/wiring
Inspect hoses for moisture
Confirm switch operation across a full call cycle
codes.escalation: Furnace will not begin ignition sequence if pressure switch fails to reopen from previous call
codes.reset_behavior: Clears once pressure switch reopens correctly and fault is removed.
codes.source: Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ flash-code meanings; value-line installation instructions blink legend

diagnostic_path: Verify twin wiring only between TWIN terminals
Confirm both IFCs are same part number
Verify transformers are in phase
Remove thermostat wires and jumper R to W to isolate thermostat/wiring fault if needed
codes.escalation: Fault blocks correct twinned operation until configuration/wiring issue is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when twinning wiring/configuration is corrected; normal operation resumes after fault removal.
codes.source: Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ twinning-fault entry; Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions troubleshooting text

platforms.platform_description: Rheem/Ruud modern 7-segment furnace control using status letters (O/C/H/F) for normal operation plus numeric codes (10-93) for faults. Covers W801T, R801T and related UT Electronic Controls 1194-250 board family. CRITICAL: code '10' is a SOFT LOCKOUT WRAPPER that alternates on the display with the underlying fault code (11, 13, 14, 61, etc.) — never read '10' as a standalone root cause. Escalation rules publicly documented: 5-minute soft lockout for low-pressure switch failing to close after 60 seconds, 1-hour soft lockout for 4 ignition failures OR 5 flame losses in one call OR undesired flame OR dead blower, hard lockout for internal control fault or welded gas-valve relay.
platforms.visual_identification: Single 7-segment alphanumeric display on integrated furnace control. Status letters shown during normal operation: O (standby), C (cooling call), H (heat call), F (continuous fan). Numeric fault codes displayed during faults — during soft lockout the display alternates between '10' (the wrapper) and the underlying fault code (11, 13, 14, 61, etc.). During lockout, normal status letters C or F are replaced by the error code.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, UT Electronic Controls 1194-250 board family. Accessible public snippet exposes the core code list plus complete lockout section. Codes 12, 26, 59 have limited per-code text in the accessible snippet — records below honestly flag this rather than padding.

diagnostic_path: If no demand exists, no action
If flame or inducer activity exists while O is shown, investigate abnormal output condition
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, UT Electronic Controls 1194-250 7-segment display family, status/fault section

root_causes: Normal cooling or heat-pump cooling demand
diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat demand and outdoor-unit operation if cooling expected
codes.escalation: During lockout, control may display the lockout error instead of C
codes.reset_behavior: Automatic with call state.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, lockout section stating control will display lockout error instead of 'C' or 'F'

diagnostic_path: Verify demand and normal ignition sequence if heat expected
codes.escalation: If a fault develops, display changes to numeric fault/lockout indication
codes.reset_behavior: Automatic with call state.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, status display family for 7-segment control

root_causes: Thermostat fan call or fan-on selection
diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat fan command and blower operation
codes.escalation: During lockout, control displays the error code instead of 'F'
codes.reset_behavior: Automatic with call state.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, lockout section

codes.meaning: One-hour soft-lockout wrapper; display alternates 10 with underlying cause code
root_causes: Five flame losses in one heat call
diagnostic_path: Identify and correct alternating underlying code (11, 13, 14, 61 etc.)
Do not treat 10 as root cause by itself
codes.escalation: One-hour soft lockout; auto-reset after one hour
codes.reset_behavior: Clears automatically after one hour, OR immediately if original fault is cleared and power is cycled, OR on soft lockout by cycling heat call off >2 sec but <20 sec.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, lockout section lines showing '10' alternates with fault causing lockout and reset behavior

root_causes: Flame not detected after ignition trials
diagnostic_path: Accessible public snippet does not expose a dedicated per-code flowchart here
Use ignition-failure checks published for corresponding furnace generation and underlying fault correlation with code 10 soft lockout
codes.escalation: Four ignition failures contribute to code 10 one-hour lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Clears with successful ignition or via soft-lockout reset procedure if wrapped by 10.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions numeric code family and one-hour lockout rule

codes.meaning: Ignition-related lockout / retry condition on this generation
root_causes: Public accessible snippet does not expose the per-code meaning text separately
Treated as part of ignition/lockout family in this generation
diagnostic_path: Use underlying ignition diagnostics
Confirm whether manual revision for exact furnace exposes 12 as retry/lockout state before publishing to production tool as customer-facing text
codes.reset_behavior: Soft-lockout reset behavior per control.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions. User-requested code list aligns with this public control generation, but exact per-code text was not exposed in the accessible snippet; lockout family and reset rules are public.

codes.meaning: Flame lost / flame failure after establishment
diagnostic_path: Accessible snippet does not expose a separate 13 chart on this exact manual page
Use flame-sense, gas pressure, grounding, and carryover checks if 13 is the underlying fault alternating with 10
codes.escalation: Five flame losses in one heat call contribute to one-hour soft lockout
codes.reset_behavior: Clears with normal flame stability or via soft-lockout reset if wrapped by 10.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, one-hour lockout triggers include five flame losses in one heat call

codes.meaning: Undesired flame / flame present when not expected
root_causes: Flame present with no commanded heat
diagnostic_path: Verify whether actual flame is present
If not, inspect flame-sense circuit and gas-valve behavior
codes.escalation: One-hour soft lockout trigger; one-hour period begins after flame is no longer detected
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-reset after one hour or standard soft-lockout reset.
codes.source: Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions, one-hour lockout trigger list includes undesired flame

root_causes: Twinning detected then lost in same power cycle
diagnostic_path: Confirm identical boards
Verify twin wiring between boards
Confirm phasing and twinning configuration
codes.escalation: Fault display until twinning issue is corrected
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when twin configuration is corrected; may require power cycle.

codes.meaning: Main limit / dead-blower main-limit-open family on this generation
diagnostic_path: Check airflow, filter, blower operation, and limit circuit continuity
codes.escalation: If main limit circuit remains open >150 seconds, dead-blower logic can contribute to one-hour or hard-lockout behavior depending count
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when limit and airflow issue are corrected; lockout resets per control rules.

platforms.platform_name: Rheem/Ruud Premium Axxx_F / Txxx_F Bluetooth Namespace
platforms.platform_description: Rheem/Ruud premium communicating/Bluetooth-capable furnace platform using Axxx_F (persistent/active faults) and Txxx_F (transient/protection events) namespace. Covers RDFXYC Bluetooth family and related premium EcoNet-capable boards. CRITICAL: Deep troubleshooting is INTENTIONALLY deferred to the Rheem Contractor App via QR code on the control panel — the installation booklet deliberately lists fault identifiers and names but does NOT print full decision trees. A technician without the Contractor App has significantly less information on this generation than on older Endeavor boards. Ignition lockout: 4 trials → 1-hour soft lockout, reset by removing power ≥5 seconds OR removing heat call.
platforms.visual_identification: Premium furnace IFC with 7-segment display PLUS Bluetooth radio. Control panel has QR code linking to Rheem Contractor App. Red LED blink-decodes the most recent fault in the fault buffer as a backup when the app is unavailable. Axxx_F codes are persistent (latched alarms); Txxx_F codes are transient (protection events that may auto-clear). Where both T and A variants exist for the same underlying condition, the T variant is transient/early-warning and the A variant is the latched alarm state after escalation.
platforms.platform_notes: Primary public source: Rheem RDFXYC Installation Instructions 92-110869-01-01, fault table and one-hour soft-lockout section. T-variants (transient) and A-variants (latched) split into separate records to preserve behavioral distinction.

platforms.platform_description: Rheem/Ruud older modulating residential gas furnace platform with 7-segment numeric-function-code display. Covers RGFD modulating family and related older modulating furnaces. Uses distinct code namespace (11, 12, 13, 14, 22/23/33 limit family, 44/45/46/57 pressure/inducer family, 67 blower motor, 82 default parameters) — NOT the same as Platform 2A Endeavor or Platform 2B Axxx_F/Txxx_F. Public diagnostic paths in the OEM manual frequently group multiple codes under the same check (e.g., 'code 22, 23, or 33: check limits'), so records below split into individual codes but honestly note where the OEM shares a diagnostic path across multiple codes.
platforms.visual_identification: Older 7-segment furnace display showing numeric function codes. Does not have Bluetooth, does not have EcoNet connection, does not alternate '10' wrapper like Endeavor. Code list and function-code troubleshooting guide printed in furnace installation manual.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Rheem RGFD Installation Instructions. Accessible public snippet exposes key codes and specific shared diagnostic paths: 'code 22, 23, or 33: check manual-reset overtemperature / limit circuit', 'code 44 or 55: check pressure switches', 'code 45 or 57: check IDM (inducer draft motor) — verify 120V at motor, run capacitor, wiring, and IFC output voltage'. Code 82 is a transient default-parameters indicator: displays for 90 seconds then stored in memory for recall. This platform demonstrates why model prefix alone is NOT sufficient to select the correct Rheem fault code dictionary.

platforms.platform_description: Rheem and Ruud EcoNet communicating platform: EcoNet Control Center smart thermostat + communicating indoor equipment (RH2T/RHMV air handlers) + communicating outdoor equipment (Prestige inverter see Platform 4). Public diagnostic coverage strongest on the air-handler side (A101-A110 + 111 codes) — thermostat-only alarm dictionary is NOT publicly exposed as a complete code list; thermostat surfaces equipment alerts via 'Service icon' flashing and 'Current Alarm' view. EcoNet bus uses R/C/E1/E2 terminals, MUST be wired daisy-chain (not star), max 125 ft between devices, 500 ft end-to-end. Control Center autodiscovers equipment within ~45 seconds of power-up. 4-minute anti-short-cycle delay after power-up or compressor shutdown.
platforms.visual_identification: EcoNet Control Center: color touchscreen smart thermostat with Wi-Fi. Shows 'Service' icon (flashes when alarm or reminder active), 'Current Alarm' view, 'Alarm History', 'Reminders' menu. Communicating air handler (RH2T/RHMV) has LED10 on control board that flashes diagnostic codes, RJ-14 diagnostic tool port, and CFM LED that flashes 1 flash per 100 CFM for airflow verification.
platforms.platform_notes: Public source: Rheem RH2T/RHMV Installation Instructions 92-20521-102 Rev. 02 (air-handler diagnostic table); Ruud Control Center Support page (thermostat surface behavior, bus topology, anti-short-cycle). LED10 flash-to-code mapping: 2 flashes=A103/A106, 3=A102, 4=A105/A104/A101, 5=A107, 6=A108, 7=A110, 8=111 no-valid-suction-temp, 10=A109, 11=111 control-fault. DIP switches ignored in communicating mode — airflow trim via thermostat or service tool.

platforms.platform_description: Rheem Prestige and Ruud Ultra inverter-driven outdoor unit platform with Universal Outdoor Control (UODC). Covers RP18AZ-class heat pumps and RA18AZ-class ACs. THREE-TIER code hierarchy: (1) Hard numeric lockouts 21L and 29L — manual reset required (power off 1 min both units), with 21L heating-mode auto-reset exception ONLY when outdoor ambient rises to -10°F; (2) A-codes — persistent/latched alarms requiring reset; (3) T-codes — transient protection events with strike-count escalation (3-strikes, 6-strikes variants). ACTIVE SYSTEM PROTECTION: inverter continuously monitors compressor power/torque/current/voltage/discharge-pressure/discharge-temperature and REDUCES SPEED before triggering hard shutdown. Rheem publishes actual SENSOR-VALIDATION MATH FORMULAS for suction and liquid transducers — contractor-level field diagnostic tool.
platforms.visual_identification: Prestige/Ultra inverter outdoor unit with UODC (Universal Outdoor Control) PCB. Unit displays hard numeric lockouts 21L (low-pressure) and 29L (high-pressure) at Control Center. A-code and T-code namespace surfaces at Control Center with _O suffix indicating outdoor unit source.
platforms.platform_notes: Primary public source: Rheem RP18AZ Installation Instructions. This is the strongest public Rheem residential inverter documentation — it exposes the full A/T code table, response actions (lockout while active / 3-strikes to permanent lockout / 3-strikes to 4-hour lockout / 3-strikes to 30-minute lockout / 6-strikes to permanent lockout), active protection exit procedure, AND sensor-validation formulas. 21L auto-reset exception is documented: only in heating mode AND only when ambient rises back to -10°F after low-ambient lockout.

root_causes: High-pressure control opened three times in same call

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Legacy soft lockout vs modern soft lockout wrapper behave identically but display differently

meanings.meaning: Legacy '1 blink' = soft lockout after 4 ignition trials or 4 flame failures in one call. Standalone display — this is the complete code.

Modern 'code 10' = soft lockout WRAPPER that alternates on the display with the underlying cause code (11 failed ignition, 13 flame lost, 14 undesired flame, 61 dead blower, etc.). NEVER read '10' as the root cause.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Same conceptual fault, different displays: legacy 1-blink is a standalone soft lockout; modern '10' is a LOCKOUT WRAPPER that alternates with the underlying fault code. On a legacy dual-LED board, 1 blink IS the answer. On a modern 7-segment Endeavor board, if you see '10', wait for the display to cycle and read the second number that alternates with it — THAT's the actual fault (11, 13, 14, 61, etc.).

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Pressure-related faults diverge massively across Rheem platforms — same concept appears as completely different codes

meanings.meaning: Legacy: 2 blinks = pressure switch circuit open; 4 blinks = pressure switch stuck closed. Broad pressure-proving diagnostics.
Endeavor: 55 stuck closed, 57 stuck open (5-min soft lockout), 58 low-pressure proving family, 59 water sensed. Granular pressure/water split.

Premium Bluetooth: A044_F low-pressure won't open (latched), T045_F low-pressure won't close (transient), T047_F/A047_F inducer 5-min lockout (transient/latched split), T055_F/A055_F/T057_F high-pressure family (two-stage only). T/A behavioral split.

Prestige inverter: 21L low-refrigerant lockout (refrigerant, NOT combustion pressure), 29L high-refrigerant lockout, A957_O/T957_O low-refrigerant 4-hour lockout, A958_O/T923_O high-refrigerant 4-hour lockout. Completely different pressure concept (refrigerant vs combustion).

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: 'Pressure fault' means something completely different across Rheem platforms. Legacy/Endeavor/Premium = COMBUSTION pressure (inducer/vent proving). Prestige inverter = REFRIGERANT pressure (charge, airflow, EXV). A tech who sees '21L' on a Prestige inverter and thinks 'pressure switch' will chase the wrong fault entirely — that's a refrigerant low-charge lockout, not a furnace combustion issue.

cross_generation_conflicts.description: Three distinct 7-segment furnace namespaces exist within Platform 2 — model prefix alone does NOT disambiguate

meanings.meaning: Endeavor (W801T/R801T UT 1194-250 boards): status O/C/H/F + numeric codes 10 wrapper, 11, 13, 14, 22, 33, 55, 57, 61, 93. 2015-2022 generation.
Premium Bluetooth (RDFXYC): Axxx_F (latched) / Txxx_F (transient) namespace — A011_F, T013_F, A014_F, A031_F, etc. Deep troubleshooting in Rheem Contractor App via QR code. 2020-2026 generation.

Older Modulating (RGFD): numeric function codes 11, 12, 13, 14, 22/23/33 limit family (shared path), 44/45/46/57 pressure-inducer family (shared path), 67 blower, 82 default-parameters. 2010-2018 generation.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: Rheem has THREE completely different 7-segment furnace code dictionaries — and the model prefix alone will NOT tell you which one you're looking at. Identify by BOARD PART NUMBER or MANUAL REVISION DATE, not model number. Endeavor uses code 10 as a wrapper alternating with the underlying fault. Premium Bluetooth uses A/T prefix codes and pushes troubleshooting into the Rheem Contractor App. Older Modulating uses standalone numeric codes with shared diagnostic paths across groups (22/23/33, 44/45/46/57).

cross_generation_conflicts.description: EcoNet event surfaces on THREE layers simultaneously — same fault has three different identifiers

meanings.meaning: Same communicating fault appears as: (1) board-level LED10 flash pattern count (2 flashes = A103/A106, 3 = A102, 4 = A104/A105/A101, etc.), (2) Control Center 'Service icon' blinking with 'Current Alarm' entry, (3) alarm-history log entry with timestamp. All three surfaces for one underlying event.

Outdoor inverter event: (1) UODC board code (Axxx_O or Txxx_O), (2) Control Center alert display (same code prefix), (3) 21L or 29L hard-lockout wrapper if escalated. Strike-count tracking determines whether T-code transient promotes to A-code latched.

cross_generation_conflicts.ui_banner_text: One EcoNet fault, three surfaces. The LED10 flash count on the equipment board tells you the raw code. The Control Center 'Service' icon tells you an alarm is active. The Alarm History log gives you timestamped sequence. Cross-reference all three — especially for intermittent issues where the Current Alarm has already cleared but the history shows the pattern.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: Rheem/Ruud has a residential electric furnace lineup (RHGH, RHKH families) but public fault code documentation for these units is essentially non-existent — like most OEMs, electric furnaces use sequencer logic without formal fault code dictionaries. Service is component-level (open element, failed sequencer, limit trip, transformer failure). On EcoNet communicating platforms, electric-heat faults surface through the communicating air handler (RH2T/RHMV) code family (A101-A110, 111) — the air handler is the primary diagnostic surface for electric heat in EcoNet systems.
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Open element on multi-stage EH packages (RHGH, RHKH families)
Failed sequencer on non-communicating air handlers
Airflow-related limit trip — surfaces as A105 (ECM fault-run) or A104 (motor no-run) on EcoNet air handlers
24V transformer or fuse failure (EcoNet would show comm loss)
Low-voltage wiring short causing repeated fuse failure
Condensate pump failure backing up into EH section (rare but documented)
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Rheem/Ruud does not have a significant manufactured-home-specific electric furnace product line. MH electric heat is typically handled through the residential air handler product lineup with downflow configurations rather than a dedicated MH line.

metadata.source_note: Compiled from public Rheem/Ruud documentation across three research passes: Rheem 90RS/90RT/90TS Installation Instructions 92-24161-66 Rev. 3 (legacy dual-LED flash legend); Rheem Gas Furnace FAQ (legacy soft-lockout causes and reset behavior); Rheem Select / W801T Installation Instructions with UT Electronic Controls 1194-250 board family (Endeavor numeric namespace with 5-min/1-hour/hard lockout rules); Rheem RDFXYC Installation Instructions 92-110869-01-01 (premium Bluetooth Axxx_F/Txxx_F namespace with QR-code contractor app integration); Rheem RGFD Installation Instructions (older modulating numeric-function-code namespace with shared diagnostic paths); Rheem RH2T/RHMV Installation Instructions 92-20521-102 Rev. 02 (EcoNet communicating air handler diagnostic table with LED10 flash-to-code mapping); Ruud Control Center Support page (thermostat-surface behavior, EcoNet bus topology rules, 4-minute anti-short-cycle, 45-second autodiscovery); Rheem RP18AZ Installation Instructions (Prestige inverter outdoor UODC with hard numeric lockouts 21L/29L, A-code latched alarms, T-code transient protection events with strike-count escalation, Active System Protection, and sensor-validation math formulas for suction/liquid transducers). Pass 1 produced methodology summary with corrected platform split (three 7-segment namespaces, not two). Pass 2 produced structured 12-field records with honest 'not explicitly detailed in accessible public snippet' flags where OEM per-code trees are deferred to the Rheem Contractor App. Pass 3 extreme deep-dive added 7 field quirks (fault-buffer behavior, 45-second autodiscovery, single-point shield grounding, no-phone-cord rule, RJ-14 diagnostic port, CFM LED flash interpretation, R-454B LDS always-powered rule), code 82 transient default-parameters indicator on older modulating, R-454B coil compatibility notes. Honest gaps: no complete public EcoNet thermostat-only alarm dictionary exists, no complete public R-454B residential fault codebook yet (packaged-unit A2L mitigation details exist but are scoped to packaged equipment), no newest-generation RP20AZ/RA20AZ detailed troubleshooting at RP18AZ depth, WeatherKing alias verification is platform-likely but not per-code verified code-by-code in public OEM sources.
